skill.name: Temporal Python Pro
skill.description: Master Temporal workflow orchestration with Python SDK. Implements durable workflows, saga patterns, and distributed transactions.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for building reliable, scalable workflow orchestration systems using Temporal Python SDK.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines. Risk score is 0/100. No suspicious patterns detected. This is a prompt-only skill providing guidance on Temporal Python SDK usage without executable code. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Build Durable Workflows with Temporal Python SDK
content.value_statement: Building reliable distributed systems is complex. This skill provides expert guidance on Temporal Python SDK for durable workflow orchestration, error handling, and production deployment.
actual_capabilities: Guide worker configuration with proper task queue setup and graceful shutdown
Design workflow patterns using @workflow.defn and @workflow.run decorators
Implement activities with sync/async execution models and ThreadPoolExecutor
Configure retry policies, timeouts, and error handling strategies
Create signal and query handlers for external workflow interaction
Set up time-skipping test environments for fast workflow validation
limitations: Cannot execute Temporal workflows or run Python code directly
Does not provide access to a running Temporal server
Cannot deploy workers or manage infrastructure
Guidance is limited to SDK patterns and best practices

use_cases.title: Microservices Orchestration
use_cases.description: Coordinate distributed transactions across multiple services using saga patterns with compensation logic.

use_cases.title: Long-Running Business Processes
use_cases.description: Implement workflows that span hours to years with automatic state persistence and recovery.
use_cases.target_user: Systems architects designing business process automation

use_cases.title: Data Processing Pipelines
use_cases.description: Build multi-stage data transformation workflows with parallel batch processing and error handling.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers creating ETL and ML pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Basic Workflow Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic Temporal workflow in Python that processes user orders. I need to validate the order, charge payment, and send a confirmation email. Show me the workflow definition and activity implementations.

prompt_templates.title: Retry Policy Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to configure retry policies for an activity that calls an external API with rate limits. The API allows 100 requests per minute and may return 429 errors. What retry policy settings should I use?

prompt_templates.title: Signal Handler Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I implement a signal handler that allows users to cancel a running workflow? Show me how to validate the signal, handle cancellation gracefully, and emit compensation activities.

prompt_templates.title: Production Testing Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to test a workflow that spans 30 days using time-skipping. Create a test that validates the workflow completes all activities in the correct order and handles timeout scenarios.

output_examples.input: Create a workflow for order fulfillment
output_examples.output: Defines a workflow with @workflow.defn, implements validate_order, charge_payment, and send_confirmation activities with proper retry policies and timeout configuration
Shows try/except pattern catching ActivityError, implementing refund_payment compensation activity, and emitting workflow cancellation events

best_practices: Keep workflows focused on orchestration logic; move all external calls to activities
Use workflow.now() and workflow.random() instead of datetime and random modules for determinism
Implement idempotent activities to ensure safe retries on transient failures
Configure appropriate timeouts: schedule_to_close for total duration, start_to_close for single attempts
Use time-skipping test environment for fast feedback on long-running workflows
anti_patterns: Using datetime.now() or random.random() directly in workflow code breaks determinism
Making direct API calls from workflows instead of using activities
Not handling ActivityError in workflows, losing ability to implement compensation
Blocking the async event loop with synchronous code in async activities
Exceeding 2MB payload size limits by passing large data between activities

faq.question: What is the difference between a workflow and an activity in Temporal?
faq.answer: Workflows define orchestration logic and must be deterministic. Activities perform external operations like API calls or database queries. Workflows call activities, which handle the actual work.

faq.question: How do I test workflows that run for days or weeks?
faq.answer: Use the WorkflowEnvironment with time-skipping enabled. This executes workflow.sleep() calls instantly, allowing you to test month-long workflows in seconds during unit tests.

faq.question: Can I update workflow code after deployment?
faq.answer: Yes, but you must maintain determinism for existing running workflows. Use workflow.get_version() to handle code changes gracefully and test with replay against production histories.

faq.question: What happens when an activity fails?
faq.answer: Temporal automatically retries based on your RetryPolicy configuration. You can specify initial interval, backoff coefficient, maximum attempts, and mark certain error types as non-retryable.

faq.question: How do signals differ from queries?
faq.answer: Signals allow external systems to send events that modify workflow state. Queries provide read-only access to workflow state for monitoring. Signals can change state; queries cannot.

faq.question: What execution model should I use for activities?
faq.answer: Use async activities for non-blocking I/O, ThreadPoolExecutor for blocking sync I/O, and ProcessPoolExecutor for CPU-intensive tasks. Never block the async event loop.
